_comment: CI variant of benchmark_config.json used by .github/workflows/test-matrix.yml: TensorSharp vs llama.cpp only, on the ggml_cuda backend, text + prefill scenarios only (no local media assets on the runner). The model registry points at files the workflow downloads from the Hugging Face repos named in each model's `_hf` field, laid out as ${model_root}/<hf-repo-name>/<file> (the `_hf` fields are documentation only — the loader ignores them; keep them in sync with the download step in the workflow). Retarget per host with the usual env overrides: BENCH_MODEL_ROOT, BENCH_LLAMA_SERVER, BENCH_TS_SERVER_DLL, BENCH_RESULTS.

text_short.description: Short single-turn text prompt (~2k-token context preamble)

text_long.description: Long-context single-turn text prompt (~2k-token context preamble + passage)

multi_turn.description: 3-turn conversation (~2k-token context preamble), timed on the final turn

json_mode.description: Structured output (response_format json_object) with ~2k-token context preamble

backends._comment: Only ggml_cuda is in defaults.backends (the CI runner). ggml_metal and mlx are registered so the same config verifies the macOS path: run with --backends ggml_metal,mlx on an Apple Silicon host (BENCH_LLAMA_SERVER pointing at a Metal llama-server build). llama.cpp has no MLX backend, so the mlx column is TensorSharp-only; the apples-to-apples reference on that host is llama.cpp on ggml_metal.
